content:
- Read startup progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, and loaded `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md` plus the `list-active-tasks` skill instructions.
- Confirmed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` contains one active candidate: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build.md` with `<passes>false</passes>` and `<priority>high</priority>`, and no visible `<blocked_by>` tag in the refreshed summary.
- Next step if interrupted: keep `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build.md` selected in `.ralph/current_task.txt` and start implementing that bug fix, then validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read startup progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed active task path is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build.md`; now reading the task body and searching CI/build files tied to qemu/buildx.
- Next step if interrupted: inspect task status for `TO BE VERIFIED` vs `NOW EXECUTE`, then either create/update the task plan and stop or execute the plan with tests.

- Read the active GHCR workflow and the earlier story-08 release-task plan that already ends in `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Determined the current bug task itself still has no `<plan>` entry, so this turn should stay in planning mode unless the task already references an execution-ready plan.
- Next step if interrupted: identify the intended third prerequisite CI job and then write `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build_plans/...` plus update the bug task with that plan path.

- Resolved the intended pipeline shape: split the serialized validation lane into separate `check` and `test` jobs, keep image builds in one matrix `build` job, and gate `publish` on those three prerequisites.
- Verified the native GitHub-hosted ARM runner label from current GitHub docs before freezing the plan; the concrete label to target is `ubuntu-24.04-arm` rather than QEMU emulation.
- Next step if interrupted: write the bug task plan file, link it from the task, end the plan with `NOW EXECUTE`, and stop so the next turn can execute the workflow change.

- Created and linked the execution-ready plan for `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build.md` at `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build_plans/2026-04-30-ci-image-pipeline-native-arm-plan.md`.
- The plan locks in the intended boundary cleanup: replace the serialized `validate` gate with parallel `check`, `test`, and matrix `build` prerequisites; remove QEMU; and run the ARM build leg on `ubuntu-24.04-arm`, with `publish` gated on all three.
- Next step if interrupted: stop here for this planning turn; on the next turn, read the task and linked plan, then execute the workflow edit and full verification (`actionlint`, `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, push, authenticated Actions run inspection).

- Read previous progress log, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries plus tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ci-image-pipeline-uses-qemu-and-serial-build.md` and prior progress says there is already an execution-ready plan.
- Next should read the task body, linked plan, and current CI workflow/tests to execute the change safely.

- Read the active bug task and its linked plan; the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must perform the workflow change and full verification.
- Confirmed the current workflow still has `validate`, `needs: validate`, and `docker/setup-qemu-action`, which matches the bug report.
- Next should inspect the full workflow file and repo status, then patch the workflow according to the execution-ready plan.

- Patched `.github/workflows/publish-ghcr.yml` to split `validate` into `check` and `test`, remove QEMU, assign `linux/arm64` to `ubuntu-24.04-arm`, and gate `publish` on `[check, test, build]`.
- This keeps the build lane independent from repo validation while preserving the digest-artifact handoff to the final manifest job.
- Next should inspect the patched workflow and run actionlint plus local repository gates.

- Verified the patched workflow file shape and ran the required local gates successfully: `actionlint`, `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed.
- The remaining acceptance gate is authenticated GitHub run inspection after pushing the workflow change.
- Next should inspect the diff/status, create the verification commit, push it, and query the resulting Actions run/jobs with `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl`.

- Created and pushed an intermediate verification commit so the workflow change could be exercised on GitHub Actions.
- The first GitHub API wrapper call failed because the wrapper expects a full API URL; no auth/token issue, just corrected invocation shape.
- Next should query the latest push run by full GitHub API URL and inspect the run/jobs for graph and native ARM evidence.

- Remote GitHub Actions verification succeeded for run `25187219706` on commit `249cec5cf3d8f13efd2b1852021d6a5c30e5cfec`.
- Evidence captured: `build` started in parallel with `check` and `test`; the ARM leg ran on `ubuntu-24.04-arm`; there was no QEMU setup step in any job; and `publish` only started after the prerequisite jobs completed.
- Next should update the bug task acceptance boxes and `<passes>true</passes>`, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, create the required final commit, push it, and stop.
